I think this is the last one and then we'll go into the release. So this is from Rob Rupp and I'm reiterating this for everyone. And this has made a huge difference. According to the guys who came to the round table, take the pictures at the end. So when you're doing your orientations for new customers, don't start with the pictures. That's how I used to do it. I didn't know this. So Rob and Trammy accidentally forgot to take the pictures in the beginning and then took them at the end and then every orientation where they took them at the end, everyone had to stay until the picture happened. And so sometimes when you're trying to sell and people are already done, they walk out the back door, they just kind of slither away, right? Whereas when they had to wait until the picture was done, they just waited and closed everyone while they were waiting for the pictures. Huge hack. Just a reminder, if you're having issues with the no sale sale show up, just charge a small deposit pay. Mail added monthly member workshops, teaching about two to three products that were related.
